_meta.note: Generated by seed/build.ts from seed/calltree.seed.ts. Seller org = Slack. Each call has its own tree (a per-prospect pruned view of the master graph); expectedValue = round(successProbability * prospect.dealValue), dealValue = seats * 180. Per-move win-rates are Beta-smoothed from 100 call outcomes.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Hey Rachel, thanks for making the time — I know you're heads-down shipping, so I'll keep this useful.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Appreciate it. We're slammed, but I carved out twenty minutes.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Perfect. Before I pitch anything — how's your team actually communicating day to day right now?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Mostly Microsoft Teams. It came bundled with our license, so that's just where everyone landed.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Totally makes sense, and to be clear I'm not here to rip Teams out — most of our customers run Slack right alongside it.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Okay, that's good to hear. So where does Slack actually win for a team like mine?

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Search and threads. Engineering decisions stop getting buried, and your incidents and deploys can pipe straight into channels.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: The buried-threads thing is real. We lose decisions in Teams all the time.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: That's exactly what we fix. What if we ran a two-week pilot with one squad — no migration, no commitment?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: One team, two weeks… yeah, that's low-risk enough that I can say yes to it.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Love it. I'll spin up the workspace and get your platform squad in today.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Let's do it. If search is as good as you say, this'll be an easy expansion conversation.

aiFeedback.summary: A disciplined coexistence play, start to finish. You refused to bash Teams, reframed to running alongside it, mirrored the buyer's own pain — buried decisions — and closed a concrete, low-risk pilot. This is the line to copy.
strengths: Didn't knock the incumbent — reframed to coexistence
Surfaced the buried-threads pain in the buyer's own words before pitching
Closed a specific, low-risk next step (a two-week one-squad pilot)
weaknesses: Left value unquantified — a dollar figure on lost decisions would have anchored EV higher
Could have set the expansion frame earlier instead of at the very end

practiceTargets.reason: The buyer asked where Slack wins — a prime moment to quantify the cost of buried decisions.
practiceTargets.drill: Tie the buried-threads pain to hours lost per week, then to dollars.

transcript.text: Everyone here already lives in Microsoft Teams.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: What breaks down in Microsoft Teams?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Finding anything later is a nightmare.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Show how decisions stay findable.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Let's get a demo on the calendar.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Set the agenda

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: How does your team chat today?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: We already use Discord.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: What's painful about Discord day-to-day?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Search is weak and threads get lost.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Tie it to Slack's search + threads.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Book me a demo.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Thanks for the time — quick agenda

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Walk me through your current setup

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: We're on Google Chat — it came with our license.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Where does Google Chat slow you down?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: We lose decisions in Google Chat constantly.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: That's exactly what search and threads fix.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: I'd want to see that live.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Thanks for taking the time to chat today, Sarah — I’m excited to discuss how Slack can help your team.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Of course, happy to chat. We’re always looking for ways to improve our processes.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Great! Can you share how your team communicates and collaborates using Microsoft Teams right now?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Well, we primarily use Teams for messaging and video calls. It’s integrated with our other Microsoft tools, so it works for us.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: I see. But have you found any challenges with it? Sometimes I hear that it can be a bit cumbersome.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: I mean, it has its quirks, but it’s part of our Microsoft license, so it’s convenient.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: I think Teams is a bit clunky and outdated — it really struggles with user experience. Slack is designed to be intuitive and user-friendly.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: I wouldn’t say it’s outdated. It’s just different. We’ve adapted to it.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: But with Slack, you get a more streamlined experience. You can easily integrate with other apps and customize your workspace.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: I understand that, but we’ve already invested in Teams and it’s working for us.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: I get that, but imagine how much more efficient your team could be with a tool that doesn’t feel like a chore to use.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Look, I appreciate your enthusiasm, but just send me some info, please.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Sure, I can do that. I just think you might be missing out on a better solution.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Thanks, but I really just want the information. I’ll review it.

aiNotes.triggerText: We already use Microsoft Teams — it's bundled with our license.
aiNotes.response: That's great to hear! Many of our customers use both Slack and Teams together. Slack excels in search capabilities, threaded conversations, and integrations with other tools, which can enhance your team's collaboration experience alongside Teams. Would you be open to exploring how Slack can complement your existing setup?

aiFeedback.summary: The rep's disparaging comments about Microsoft Teams immediately after the buyer mentioned it as their current solution caused the buyer to become defensive, leading to a loss of interest in continuing the conversation. This reaction ultimately derailed the opportunity to further explore Slack's value proposition.
strengths: Effective discovery question to understand the buyer's current situation.
weaknesses: Disparaging remarks about the incumbent solution alienated the buyer.
Failed to pivot the conversation positively after the incumbent objection.

practiceTargets.reason: Disparaging the incumbent made the buyer defensive and closed off further discussion.
practiceTargets.drill: Practice reframing negative comments into constructive comparisons or questions that invite dialogue.

practiceTargets.reason: This was a critical moment to acknowledge the buyer's current solution and position Slack as a complementary option.
practiceTargets.drill: Practice acknowledging the incumbent's strengths while highlighting Slack's unique benefits.
